title: Fully Funded PhD Position in Social Anthropology at the University of Bergen, Norway (2026) | Salary of NOK 593,700 Per Year
summary: The University of Bergen (UiB) in Norway is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD position in Social Anthropology at its prestigious Department of Social Anthropology . This four-year doctoral appointment offers outstanding researchers the opportunity to pursue cutting-edge anthropological research while receiving an annual salary of NOK 593,700 , automatic yearly salary increases, excellent welfare benefits, and valuable teaching and academic experience. Located in Bergen, Norway , the Department of Social Anthropology is one of Europe s largest and most internationally recognized anthropology departments. The successful candidate will become part of a vibrant research community comprising internationally renowned scholars, postdoctoral researchers, and doctoral candidates conducting ethnographic research across the globe. If you are passionate about anthropology and aspire to build an academic or research career at one of Europe s leading universities, this opportunity provides an excellent platform to advance your research while contributing to teaching, supervision, and scholarly engagement. Application Deadline Deadline: 15 September 2026 Applications submitted after the deadline or without the required research proposal will not be considered. About the University of Bergen The University of Bergen (UiB) is one of Norway s leading research universities and is internationally recognized for excellence in research, education, and innovation. The university aims to contribute to sustainable and democratic societal development through high-quality teaching and research while maintaining an inclusive, collaborative, and internationally oriented academic environment. Its Faculty of Social Sciences is home to several internationally respected research groups and offers doctoral education across diverse disciplines. About the Department of Social Anthropology The Department of Social Anthropology is among the largest anthropology departments in Europe and offers exceptional opportunities for doctoral researchers. The department currently includes: 17 permanent academic staff members. 5 postdoctoral researchers. Approximately 25 PhD candidates. Researchers conduct ethnographic studies in nearly every region of the world while contributing to internationally funded research projects. The department also hosts: Monthly research seminars featuring international scholars. The prestigious Fredrik Barth Memorial Lecture. Active public engagement and academic outreach activities. Teaching at Bachelor s, Master s, and PhD levels. This intellectually stimulating environment allows doctoral candidates to collaborate with leading anthropologists while developing their own independent research. About the PhD Position The successful applicant will be employed for four years . The appointment consists of: 75% doctoral research 25% teaching, supervision, and administrative duties This structure enables candidates to gain valuable university teaching experience alongside their doctoral studies, preparing them for future academic careers. The selected candidate will be based in Bergen, Norway , and is expected to be physically present at the department except during approved fieldwork or research travel. PhD Programme Successful applicants will be admitted to the PhD Programme at the Faculty of Social Sciences. The doctoral programme normally includes: Three years of structured doctoral research. Coursework and academic training. Supervision by experienced researchers. Research seminars. Professional development opportunities. International collaboration. Applicants should possess educational qualifications equivalent to: A Bachelor s degree (180 ECTS). A Master s degree (120 ECTS). Alternatively, applicants with integrated five-year Master s programmes totaling approximately 300 ECTS are also eligible. The Master s degree should include an independent research thesis of at least 30 ECTS. Salary and Benefits Successful candidates will receive an attractive employment package, including: Annual salary of NOK 593,700 . Automatic annual salary increases of approximately 3% during the four-year appointment. Membership in the Norwegian Public Service Pension Fund. Comprehensive welfare benefits. A supportive and internationally recognized research environment. Excellent career development opportunities. Teaching and supervision experience. Access to world-class academic resources. Opportunities to collaborate with internationally renowned anthropologists. Research Proposal Requirements Applicants must submit a research proposal of 5–8 pages , including references. The proposal should clearly explain: Research topic. Research questions. Theoretical framework. Research methodology. Project objectives. Timeline and progress plan. Expected contribution to anthropology. Preference will be given to proposals that complement the department s existing regional and thematic strengths while expanding its research profile. The quality of the proposal will play a central role in the admission process. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Hold a Master s degree (or equivalent) in Anthropology. Complete the degree before the application deadline. Normally have achieved a Grade B or better overall and on the Master s thesis. Demonstrate academic independence. Possess excellent organizational skills. Work effectively both independently and collaboratively. Demonstrate excellent written and spoken English proficiency. Experience in teaching, academic outreach, or public dissemination is considered an advantage but is not mandatory. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: Cover letter outlining research interests and motivation. Research proposal (5–8 pages). Curriculum Vitae (CV). Bachelor s and Master s transcripts. Degree certificates. Academic references. Contact details for two referees. Master s thesis. List of academic publications. Publications submitted for assessment. Applicants whose qualifications were obtained outside the Nordic countries may later be asked to provide additional documentation regarding course syllabi. Applications must be submitted through the Jobbnorge recruitment portal. Applications sent by email will not be accepted. Apply Here Working Environment The Department offers an inspiring academic culture characterized by: International collaboration. Interdisciplinary research. Active research seminars. Guest lectures by international scholars. Mentorship from experienced faculty. Strong research networks. Public engagement opportunities. The university values diversity and inclusion and encourages applications regardless of gender, age, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or background. Applicants with disabilities, immigrant backgrounds, or career gaps are particularly encouraged to apply. Why You Should Apply This fully funded PhD represents an exceptional opportunity for aspiring anthropologists seeking to conduct internationally recognized research while gaining valuable teaching experience. With competitive funding, excellent academic supervision, access to a vibrant research community, and the opportunity to work at one of Europe s leading universities, the programme provides an ideal foundation for a successful academic or research career. Interested candidates should prepare a strong research proposal and submit their complete application before 15 September 2026 . For more information on this PhD position, visit here Discover more PhD opportunities on OFY, here The post Fully Funded PhD Position in Social Anthropology at the University of Bergen, Norway (2026) | Salary of NOK 593,700 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AAN Research Program 2027: Up to $450,000 in Research Funding for Early-Career Neurologists and Neuroscience Researchers
summary: The American Academy of Neurology (AAN) has officially opened applications for its 2027 AAN Research Program , offering grants ranging from $150,000 to $450,000 to support groundbreaking neuroscience research. The funding program is designed to empower early-career neurologists and neuroscience researchers pursuing innovative research aimed at improving the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of neurological disorders. Through these competitive awards, AAN continues its commitment to advancing neuroscience, supporting emerging researchers, and accelerating discoveries that improve the lives of people living with diseases affecting the brain and nervous system. Researchers with promising ideas and a passion for transforming neurological care are encouraged to submit their applications before the closing date. Application Deadline Deadline: September 10, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully review the eligibility requirements for each funding opportunity. About the AAN Research Program The AAN Research Program is one of the American Academy of Neurology s flagship initiatives dedicated to supporting neuroscience research and cultivating the next generation of research leaders. The program provides substantial funding that enables researchers to: Launch independent research careers. Develop innovative scientific projects. Generate preliminary data for larger national grants. Build research laboratories. Recruit research personnel. Advance discoveries in neurological disease prevention and treatment. Improve patient outcomes through scientific innovation. The grants are specifically intended to help researchers establish long-term careers while contributing meaningful advances in neurology. Funding Available Applicants may compete for research awards ranging between: $150,000 $450,000 Funding amounts vary depending on the specific grant category. Many awards provide support over two years, while larger Career Development Awards provide funding over three years. Research Areas Supported The AAN Research Program welcomes innovative proposals focused on: Prevention of neurological diseases. Diagnosis of neurological disorders. Treatment of brain diseases. Disorders affecting the nervous system. Clinical neurology research. Translational neuroscience. Healthcare delivery and quality improvement. Neuroscience innovations. Patient-centered neurological care. Projects with strong scientific merit and the potential to improve neurological care are highly encouraged. Grant Categories The AAN Research Program offers several funding opportunities tailored to different career stages. Early-Career Research Awards Many grants provide: Two-year funding. Awards valued at $150,000 . Support for neurologists and neuroscience researchers one to five years into their careers. These grants help recipients establish research independence and generate data for future funding opportunities. Career Development Awards The Career Development Awards provide: Three years of funding. Awards of $450,000 . Support for neurologists with six to ten years of professional experience. These awards are designed to accelerate long-term research careers while positioning recipients for future national and international research funding. Why This Funding Matters Research funding plays a critical role in advancing neuroscience and improving patient care. Recipients use the awards to: Establish research laboratories. Protect dedicated research time. Hire research staff. Generate preliminary scientific data. Publish scientific findings. Develop innovative treatment approaches. Strengthen future grant applications. Build sustainable research programs. Many previous award recipients have gone on to receive major national research funding after benefiting from the AAN Research Program. Success Story: Advancing Multiple Sclerosis Research One notable recipient, Dr. Cole A. Harrington , received the 2024 Career Development Award for research investigating oligodendrocyte survival and remyelination in multiple sclerosis. According to Dr. Harrington, the award enabled him to: Launch his research laboratory. Protect dedicated research time. Recruit essential laboratory staff. Generate valuable preliminary data. Develop promising treatment pathways. Produce multiple scientific publications. He described the award as a launchpad that significantly strengthened his research career and future scientific opportunities. Success Story: Addressing Health Care Disparities Another recipient, Dr. Altaf Saadi , received the Practice Research Training Scholarship to investigate healthcare disparities affecting patients with limited English proficiency. Her research revealed important differences in neurological care, including variations in diagnostic testing and communication barriers between patients and healthcare providers. The funding also helped her build a successful research career that later led to receiving an NIH K23 award, highlighting the long-term career impact of AAN research funding. Benefits of the AAN Research Program Recipients benefit from numerous career-building opportunities, including: Significant research funding. Career development support. Increased research independence. Strong mentorship opportunities. Enhanced scientific credibility. Opportunities to publish research findings. Improved competitiveness for larger grants. Expanded professional networks. Long-term academic career growth. Greater impact on neurological patient care. Advice from Previous Award Recipients Past awardees shared valuable insights for future applicants. Build a Strong Mentorship Team Applicants are encouraged to: Seek guidance from experienced faculty. Build multidisciplinary mentorship committees. Consult previous award recipients. Meet regularly with mentors to strengthen research proposals. Strong mentorship can improve project quality and help overcome research challenges. Be Persistent Applicants who are unsuccessful on their first attempt are encouraged to reapply. Receiving reviewer feedback, improving proposals, and maintaining persistence can significantly increase the likelihood of future success. Tips for Preparing a Competitive Application To improve your chances of success: Develop a clearly defined research question. Demonstrate innovation and scientific significance. Explain the potential impact on neurological care. Build a strong mentorship team. Present a realistic research timeline. Clearly outline your career development goals. Highlight previous research accomplishments. Carefully follow all application instructions. Why You Should Apply The AAN Research Program represents one of the leading funding opportunities for early-career neurologists and neuroscience researchers. Whether you are launching your first independent research project or expanding an established programme, these grants provide the financial resources, mentorship opportunities, and professional recognition needed to build a successful research career while advancing neuroscience and improving patient care worldwide. Interested applicants should review the programme guidelines and submit their applications before September 10, 2026 . Apply Here For more information on this grant, visit here For more grant opportunities on OFY, click here The post AAN Research Program 2027: Up to $450,000 in Research Funding for Early-Career Neurologists and Neuroscience Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNDP Communication and Social Media Internship 2026 in Copenhagen, Denmark | Monthly Stipend of Approximately $1,000
summary: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) is inviting applications for its Communication and Social Media Internship based in Copenhagen, Denmark . This six-month full-time internship presents an outstanding opportunity for students and recent graduates who are passionate about communications, digital storytelling, social media, and international development. Successful applicants will join the People Programmes and Partnerships (PPP) Unit within UNDP s Office of Human Resources, where they will contribute to employer branding, talent attraction initiatives, digital communications, and social media campaigns that support UNDP s global recruitment efforts. Interns receive a monthly stipend equivalent to approximately USD 1,000 (subject to confirmation), gain valuable professional experience within one of the world s leading international development organizations, participate in professional training programmes, and collaborate with experts working across more than 170 countries and territories . Application Deadline Deadline: 12 July 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the closing date. About UNDP The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) is the United Nations global development network dedicated to helping countries eradicate poverty, reduce inequality, strengthen institutions, and achieve sustainable development. Operating in approximately 170 countries and territories , UNDP works closely with governments, development partners, civil society organizations, and communities to implement solutions that advance the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development and the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). As part of its People for 2030 Strategy , UNDP is investing heavily in attracting and developing talented professionals capable of addressing today s complex global challenges. About the Internship The Communication and Social Media Intern will support the People Programmes and Partnerships (PPP) team, which is responsible for: Managing young talent programmes. Supporting employer branding initiatives. Developing recruitment strategies. Enhancing candidate experiences. Promoting UNDP careers globally. Supporting talent acquisition. Improving recruitment communication. The internship is designed for individuals who are interested in combining communications with international development while gaining practical experience in a multicultural environment. Internship Location Copenhagen, Denmark Internship Duration Six (6) months Full-time The selected intern is expected to begin around 1 September , although flexibility is available for remote or in-house training before the official start date. Monthly Stipend UNDP provides interns with a monthly stipend intended to help cover internship-related living costs. For Copenhagen, the stipend is approximately: USD 1,000 per month (subject to confirmation) The exact amount may vary depending on whether the intern receives financial support from another institution or government. Key Responsibilities The internship combines communication, social media management, analytics, storytelling, and research. Communication and Social Media (Approximately 85%) Interns will: Manage UNDP Careers social media platforms. Publish engaging content across social media channels. Respond to comments, questions, and audience engagement. Help develop social media content strategies. Identify compelling employee stories and success testimonials. Produce engaging digital storytelling content. Collect communication performance data. Monitor Key Performance Indicators (KPIs). Analyse social media performance. Prepare communication reports for external partners. Support employer branding initiatives. Social media platforms include: LinkedIn Facebook Instagram X (formerly Twitter) Research and Administrative Support (Approximately 15%) Interns will also: Conduct research on communication best practices. Support ongoing PPP projects. Assist with additional assignments as required. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for individuals who enjoy: Social media management Digital communications Employer branding Content creation Storytelling International development Human resources communications Data analytics Visual communications Digital marketing Educational Requirements Applicants must satisfy one of the following: Be in the final year of a Bachelor s degree. Be currently enrolled in a Master s programme. Have completed a Master s degree within the previous one year. Eligible Fields of Study Suitable academic backgrounds include: Communications Digital Media Journalism Political Science Public Relations Media Studies Marketing International Relations Other related disciplines Desired Skills and Experience Although not mandatory, applicants with the following qualifications will have an advantage: Previous experience managing social media platforms. Experience producing digital content. Familiarity with Adobe Creative Cloud. Skills in: Adobe InDesign Adobe Illustrator Adobe Premiere Pro Strong writing abilities. Excellent communication skills. Analytical thinking. Content planning. Creativity. Organizational skills. Language Requirements Required: Fluency in English. Preferred: Working knowledge of French. Working knowledge of Spanish. Learning Opportunities Interns become integrated members of the PPP Team and benefit from significant professional development opportunities. These include: In-house training programmes. UNDP online learning courses. Exposure to UNDP s organizational structure. Practical experience with international recruitment. Professional writing development. Visual communication training. Participation in working groups. Collaboration with international professionals. Access to an in-house gym membership. Internship Benefits Successful interns will enjoy numerous professional and personal development benefits, including: Monthly stipend. Practical international work experience. Networking opportunities. Professional mentorship. Participation in UNDP training. Exposure to global recruitment strategies. Employer branding experience. Social media campaign experience. International teamwork. Access to modern workplace facilities. Gym membership. Paid leave of 1.5 days per month. Official UN holidays. Internship Conditions Applicants should carefully note the following conditions: Interns are not considered UNDP staff members. Interns cannot officially represent UNDP. Full-time participation is expected. Flexibility may be granted for academic commitments. Interns must arrange: Accommodation Travel Visa (if applicable) Living expenses beyond the stipend Health insurance coverage is mandatory. UNDP accepts no responsibility for medical or travel-related costs. Importantly, this internship is only available to individuals who already possess the legal right to live and work in Denmark. Diversity and Inclusion UNDP is committed to promoting: Diversity Equity Inclusion Equal opportunity Fair recruitment Applicants from marginalized and underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. UNDP maintains zero tolerance for: Sexual exploitation Sexual harassment Abuse of authority Workplace discrimination All successful applicants will undergo reference and background checks. Important Notice UNDP never charges applicants any fees during the recruitment process. Applicants should ignore anyone requesting payment for: Applications Interviews Recruitment Processing Training Testing Why You Should Apply If you aspire to build an international career in communications, employer branding, digital media, or development communications, this internship provides exceptional exposure to one of the world s most respected international organizations. You will strengthen your communication skills, gain hands-on social media experience, work with professionals from diverse cultures, and contribute to attracting global talent to support sustainable development worldwide. Apply Here For more information on this internship visit here For more internship opportunities on OFY click here The post UNDP Communication and Social Media Internship 2026 in Copenhagen, Denmark | Monthly Stipend of Approximately $1,000 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Conservation Nation 2026 Grant Program: Up to US$5,000 in Funding for Early- to Mid-Career Conservationists Worldwide
summary: Wildlife conservation is one of the most urgent global priorities of our time. Around the world, biodiversity is under increasing pressure due to habitat destruction, climate change, pollution, illegal wildlife trade, invasive species, and unsustainable resource use. Addressing these complex challenges requires dedicated conservation leaders who understand local ecosystems and can develop sustainable, community-driven solutions. The Conservation Nation 2026 Grant Program has been established to support exactly these leaders. Through this competitive grant initiative, Conservation Nation will provide funding and long-term professional support to conservationists working to protect threatened wildlife species, conserve biodiversity, restore habitats, and strengthen ecosystem resilience. In 2026, eight early- to mid-career conservationists will each receive grants of up to US$5,000 to implement impactful conservation projects that benefit wildlife, ecosystems, and local communities. Beyond financial support, successful applicants will become part of the Conservation Nation Network , gaining access to mentorship, professional development, capacity-building opportunities, and valuable connections within the global conservation community. About the Conservation Nation Grant Program The Conservation Nation Grant Program is designed to strengthen wildlife conservation by investing directly in conservation professionals who are creating meaningful change within their communities. Rather than focusing solely on funding projects, Conservation Nation also focuses on empowering the people behind conservation efforts. The organization believes that sustainable conservation outcomes are achieved when passionate, knowledgeable, diverse, and locally connected conservationists are equipped with the resources they need to lead. The program particularly values leadership from communities whose voices and perspectives have historically been underrepresented in conservation. By combining financial support with ongoing professional development, Conservation Nation seeks to create lasting conservation impact across terrestrial, freshwater, and marine ecosystems worldwide. Grant Value Successful applicants will receive: Grants of up to US$5,000 Membership in the Conservation Nation Network Professional development opportunities Capacity-building support Networking opportunities with conservation professionals Access to conservation resources Continued mentorship and support after receiving the grant Number of Grants Available The 2026 program will award funding to: Eight conservationists Purpose of the Grant The grant program supports initiatives that contribute to: Wildlife conservation Biodiversity protection Habitat preservation Ecosystem stewardship Community engagement Long-term biodiversity resilience Conservation leadership development The program recognizes that protecting wildlife and ecosystems requires both scientific knowledge and strong community engagement. It therefore supports conservation projects that involve local stakeholders and generate sustainable environmental outcomes. Types of Projects Supported Conservation Nation welcomes innovative, community-informed conservation initiatives from around the world. Preference will be given to projects involving: Field research Wildlife conservation Biodiversity conservation Habitat restoration Ecosystem management Community-led conservation Conservation education Ecosystem stewardship Importantly, applicants may also request funding for activities that are essential to advancing their conservation work but are often overlooked by traditional funding mechanisms. This flexible approach allows conservationists to secure support for critical components of their work that may otherwise struggle to obtain funding. Why This Grant Is Different Many conservation grants focus exclusively on project expenses. Conservation Nation takes a broader approach by investing in conservation professionals themselves. Recipients benefit from: Flexible funding Long-term professional support Access to a global conservation network Capacity-building opportunities Leadership development Ongoing mentorship Professional connections This integrated support system helps conservationists expand both their projects and their long-term careers. Conservation Nation Network Benefits Grant recipients automatically become members of the Conservation Nation Network. Network members receive: Professional development opportunities Continued learning resources Collaboration opportunities Peer networking Capacity-building support Connections with fellow conservation professionals Resources to strengthen future conservation initiatives The network is designed to ensure that grantees continue growing long after their funded projects have been completed. Funding Priorities The grant program seeks projects that contribute to: Protection of threatened wildlife species Conservation of biodiversity Habitat preservation Habitat restoration Ecosystem conservation Sustainable ecosystem management Community participation in conservation Conservation leadership Biodiversity resilience Projects that combine scientific research with meaningful community engagement are particularly encouraged. Who Should Apply? The opportunity is ideal for conservation professionals who are already working to improve biodiversity and ecosystem health. Applicants may include those working in: Wildlife conservation Ecology Biodiversity conservation Environmental science Marine conservation Freshwater conservation Habitat restoration Community conservation Natural resource management Early- and mid-career professionals seeking financial support and long-term career development are especially encouraged to apply. How to Prepare Before Applications Open Prospective applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their application materials well before the portal opens. Preparation should include: Confirming eligibility requirements Preparing a detailed project summary Developing a realistic project budget Creating a project timeline Recording the required Role Model Video Gathering supporting documents Obtaining any necessary approvals Preparing these materials in advance can significantly improve the quality of the final application. Important Application Information Applicants should note the following: Applications must be submitted through the official grant portal. Applications sent via email will not be considered. Applicants are encouraged to review all application questions before submitting. The application portal serves as the final authority on application instructions and requirements. Requirements may be updated before the application period officially opens. Application Timeline Applicants should carefully observe the official application schedule. Applications Open: July 8, 2026, at 10:00 AM EST Application Deadline: July 15, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST The application window will remain open for one week , making early preparation especially important. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official Conservation Nation Grant Application Portal once it opens. Applicants can also: Register an account in advance. Review the application questions before submission. Download the preview of application questions. Explore previous Conservation Nation grantees for inspiration. Contact the grants team for questions. Application portal: Apply Here Final Thoughts The Conservation Nation 2026 Grant Program represents an outstanding opportunity for conservation professionals committed to protecting wildlife and biodiversity. With grants of up to US$5,000 , ongoing professional development, and access to an international conservation network, the program offers much more than financial assistance. If you are leading innovative conservation work, particularly one rooted in local communities and aimed at protecting wildlife and ecosystems, this grant provides an excellent platform to expand your impact while advancing your professional journey.Be sure to prepare your materials early and submit your application before the July 15, 2026, deadline . For more information visit here 2026-grant-program-application-information Dsicover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Conservation Nation 2026 Grant Program: Up to US$5,000 in Funding for Early- to Mid-Career Conservationists Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNICEF Programme Officer (P-1) Job in Thailand 2026: Apply for the Climate Resilience for Children Position in Bangkok
summary: For professionals seeking an opportunity to build an international career while making a lasting impact on children s lives, the UNICEF Programme Officer (P-1) position in Bangkok presents an exceptional opportunity. UNICEF is recruiting a Programme Officer (P-1) on a 364-day temporary appointment within its Global Programme Division , supporting the Climate Resilience for Children initiative under the Healthy Environments for Healthy Children (HEHC) Programme . Based in Bangkok, Thailand , this role places successful candidates at the heart of UNICEF s global efforts to protect children from environmental threats, including climate change, pollution, and unhealthy built environments. Working alongside international experts, country offices, governments, and development partners, the Programme Officer will help strengthen programme delivery, financial management, knowledge sharing, and coordination across UNICEF s global network. About UNICEF UNICEF operates in more than 190 countries and territories , working tirelessly to protect children s rights, improve health and nutrition, promote education, strengthen child protection systems, and respond to humanitarian crises. The organization believes every child deserves the opportunity to survive, thrive, and reach their full potential regardless of where they are born. Employees at UNICEF become part of a diverse international workforce committed to delivering meaningful change while enjoying opportunities for professional growth, international exposure, and continuous learning. About the Position Position: Programme Officer (P-1) Contract Type: Temporary Appointment Duration: 364 days Duty Station: Bangkok, Thailand Division: Global Programme Division (GPD) Programme: Healthy Environments for Healthy Children (HEHC) Focus Area: Climate Resilience for Children The Programme Officer will provide operational, administrative, financial, and coordination support to ensure effective implementation of global programmes protecting children s health from environmental hazards. About the Healthy Environments for Healthy Children Programme The HEHC Programme focuses on improving children s health by reducing exposure to environmental risks such as: Climate change Air pollution Environmental contamination Unsafe built environments Other environmental hazards affecting child development The programme also supports the Children s Environmental Health Collaborative , bringing together global expertise to improve child health outcomes. Key Responsibilities Successful candidates will contribute to several core programme functions. 1. Global Programme Management The Programme Officer will support the implementation of international programmes by: Monitoring programme progress Tracking implementation milestones Supporting donor-funded projects Coordinating programme activities across multiple countries Ensuring compliance with UNICEF policies 2. Financial Management Financial oversight forms an important part of the role, including: Budget tracking Financial monitoring Budget transfers Monitoring expenditure Supporting donor reporting Maintaining financial records using UNICEF s VISION ERP system 3. Procurement and Contract Management Responsibilities include: Procuring technical expertise Supporting recruitment of consultants Managing contracts Processing procurement documentation Coordinating technical assistance deployment 4. Administrative Coordination The Programme Officer will also coordinate day-to-day programme operations through: Organizing meetings Managing travel arrangements Supporting administrative workflows Coordinating country office communications Maintaining programme documentation 5. Knowledge Management Knowledge-sharing responsibilities include: Managing digital collaboration platforms Supporting SharePoint systems Organizing programme information Facilitating collaboration across UNICEF offices Maintaining institutional knowledge resources Required Qualifications Applicants should possess a bachelor s degree or equivalent in one of the following areas: Social Sciences International Relations Public Administration Government Public Policy Social Policy Social Development Community Development International Business Relations Other related disciplines Required Professional Experience Applicants must have at least one year of relevant professional experience in areas such as: Programme implementation Administration Coordination Financial tracking Procurement Administrative services Programme support Required Technical Skills Strong candidates should demonstrate experience with: Budget monitoring Financial reporting Procurement procedures Contract administration Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems Data management Digital collaboration tools SharePoint or similar platforms Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Fluency in English Additional knowledge of any UN official language is considered an advantage: French Arabic Chinese Russian Spanish Desired Experience UNICEF particularly values candidates with experience in: International organizations United Nations agencies Humanitarian response Development programmes Fragile settings Executive support Programme administration Human resources processes Financial management UNICEF Core Competencies Successful applicants are expected to demonstrate the organization s CRITAS values: Care Respect Integrity Trust Accountability Sustainability Candidates should also demonstrate competencies including: Building partnerships Strategic thinking Innovation Collaboration Managing complexity Delivering results Ethical awareness Diversity and Inclusion UNICEF is committed to maintaining an inclusive workforce and strongly encourages applications from qualified candidates regardless of: Nationality Gender Race Ethnicity Religion Disability Sexual orientation Socio-economic background Reasonable accommodation is provided throughout the recruitment process for persons with disabilities. Benefits of Working with UNICEF Joining UNICEF offers numerous professional advantages, including: International career development Competitive compensation package Professional learning opportunities Global networking Diverse multicultural workplace Paid parental leave Breastfeeding breaks Disability accommodations Opportunities to contribute to global humanitarian and development initiatives Important Recruitment Information Applicants should note that: UNICEF does not charge any recruitment or application fees . Background and reference checks are mandatory. Medical clearance may be required. Visa support will be provided where applicable. Vaccination requirements may apply according to UNICEF policies. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Why You Should Apply This Programme Officer position offers an excellent entry point into an international career with one of the world s leading humanitarian organizations. Beyond administrative and programme support responsibilities, the role allows professionals to contribute directly to global efforts addressing climate resilience, environmental health, and child well-being. Working in Bangkok within UNICEF s Global Programme Division provides valuable exposure to international programme management, donor coordination, climate policy, and multi-country development initiatives. Application Deadline The specific application deadline was not stated in the information provided. Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications as soon as possible through the official UNICEF Careers portal. title: Preparing for Tomorrow 2026 Grant: International Research Funding Opportunity for Social Sciences and Humanities Researchers
summary: The Trans-Atlantic Platform (T-AP) has officially launched its fifth international research funding call titled “Preparing for Tomorrow – Societies and Strategies in Times of Transition.” The initiative seeks to support innovative and collaborative research aimed at helping societies understand, anticipate, and respond to future challenges in an increasingly uncertain world. As the world grapples with climate change, pandemics, geopolitical tensions, technological disruptions, economic instability, and emerging social challenges, there is a growing need for evidence-based strategies that can help communities and policymakers prepare for the future. Through this funding call, T-AP aims to strengthen international cooperation among researchers and promote interdisciplinary solutions to complex global issues. About the Trans-Atlantic Platform (T-AP) The Trans-Atlantic Platform for Social Sciences and Humanities is a large-scale collaboration involving research funding agencies from Europe, North America, South America, and Africa. The platform promotes international dialogue and collaborative research to address common societal challenges through social sciences and humanities research. The Preparing for Tomorrow initiative represents the fifth joint call organized by T-AP and focuses on building resilient and inclusive societies capable of navigating uncertain futures. Aim of the Grant The program will support collaborative research projects that advance understanding of how societies: Envision future scenarios. Prepare for emerging crises. Respond to uncertainty. Develop strategies for resilience and sustainable development. Inform policy and decision-making processes at local, national, and international levels. Projects are expected to generate knowledge that contributes to forward-looking and inclusive responses to societal transitions. Research Themes Applicants are invited to address one or more of the following themes: 1. Understanding Uncertainty Research may examine: Sources of uncertainty. Economic and social costs. Communication of risks. Strategies for reducing uncertainty. 2. Futures and Crises Researchers may explore: Historical perspectives on crises. Cultural interpretations of the future. Regional differences in preparing for change. Social responses to emerging threats. 3. Response Strategies and Coordination Projects may investigate: Governance systems. Coordination mechanisms. Public policy responses. Institutional preparedness. 4. Prevention and Preparation Research may focus on: Ethical and normative questions. Long-term preparedness. Prevention frameworks. Responsibility and accountability in crisis management. Key Features of the Grant The program aims to: Support international and interdisciplinary research collaboration. Promote engagement with communities and policymakers. Encourage participation and development of early-career researchers. Foster diversity within research teams. Address opportunities and risks associated with technological, political, environmental, and societal change. Eligible Disciplines The call welcomes proposals employing: Qualitative research methods. Quantitative approaches. Mixed-methods research. Historical studies. Future-oriented methodologies. Interdisciplinary approaches within Social Sciences and Humanities. Consortium Requirements Applications must involve: At least three eligible principal investigators. Participants from at least three different participating countries. Representation from both sides of the Atlantic. Compliance with national funding regulations. Applicants are encouraged to consult the national addenda provided by their funding organizations to confirm eligibility. Duration of Projects Funded projects are expected to run for: Between 24 and 36 months. Benefits of the Preparing for Tomorrow Grant Successful applicants will gain: Access to international research funding. Opportunities for transnational collaboration. Engagement with policymakers and stakeholders. Increased visibility of research outputs. Capacity-building opportunities. Support for interdisciplinary innovation. Opportunities for early-career researchers to develop internationally. Important Dates Call Launch 17 April 2026 Letter of Intent Deadline 8 July 2026 Full Application Deadline 28 October 2026 Evaluation Period March – April 2027 Funding Decisions Announced April – May 2027 Expected Start of Awards July – September 2027 How to Apply Applications are submitted through the German Research Foundation (DFG) Elan Portal by the Lead Principal Investigator on behalf of the consortium. Researchers should: Build an international consortium. Review the call scope and national addenda. Submit the Letter of Intent before 8 July 2026. Prepare and submit the full proposal by 28 October 2026. Ensure compliance with requirements established by their national funding agencies. Applicants can access the call guidelines and application documents here The Preparing for Tomorrow Grant represents a significant opportunity for social sciences and humanities researchers seeking to contribute to global preparedness and resilience. Through international collaboration and interdisciplinary approaches, the program seeks to generate new knowledge and strategies that will help societies navigate uncertainty and build a more sustainable future. title: WomensNet 2026 Grants: Apply for More Than $510,000 in Funding for Women-Owned Businesses Including $10,000 Monthly Grants and $50,000 Annual Grants
summary: Women entrepreneurs often face significant challenges when trying to secure funding to launch, grow, or scale their businesses. To bridge this gap, WomensNet continues its long-standing commitment to supporting women in business through its 2026 WomensNet Grants Program , offering more than $510,000 in grant funding throughout the year . One of the biggest advantages of the WomensNet grant program is its simple application process. Instead of submitting multiple applications for different grant opportunities, applicants complete one application and are automatically considered for all grants for which they qualify. Whether you are still developing your business idea, running a startup, growing an established company, or leading a nonprofit organization, the WomensNet grants provide multiple funding opportunities throughout the year. Total Funding Available The WomensNet 2026 Grants Program provides more than US$510,000 in funding through: Monthly Amber Grants Monthly Startup Grants Monthly Business Category Grants Annual Amber Grants Successful applicants may even become eligible for additional year-end funding worth US$50,000 . Why Apply? Applicants only need to complete one application to be considered for multiple grant opportunities. Benefits include: More than US$510,000 available annually. One application covers all eligible grants. Monthly funding opportunities. Additional chances to receive year-end grants. Support for startups and established businesses. Nonprofit organizations are also eligible. Wide variety of industry-specific grants. 1. Monthly Amber Grant The flagship WomensNet funding opportunity is the Monthly Amber Grant . Each month, one outstanding woman-owned business receives: US$10,000 grant Eligible businesses include: Small businesses Established businesses Growing businesses Women-led nonprofit organizations Businesses from all industries Applications are carefully reviewed each month before one winner is selected. Additional Benefit Every Monthly Amber Grant recipient automatically advances to compete for the: US$50,000 Annual Amber Grant This provides successful applicants with an opportunity to secure even larger funding at the end of the year. 2. Monthly Startup Grant Recognizing that early-stage businesses often struggle the most to access funding, WomensNet also offers the Monthly Startup Grant . Each month: One startup receives US$10,000 . WomensNet defines a startup as: A business still in the idea stage, or A business with less than US$10,000 in sales . Although startups are already eligible for the Monthly Amber Grant, WomensNet created this additional funding opportunity specifically to support entrepreneurs at the earliest stages of business development. Additional Benefit Monthly Startup Grant winners also become eligible for the: US$50,000 Annual Startup Grant 3. Business Category Grants Women entrepreneurs operating within specific industries may qualify for the Business Category Grants . Each category awards: US$10,000 One of the most attractive features of this program is that once an applicant submits an Amber Grant application, it remains active for consideration under the relevant business category for 12 months . There is no need to submit another application . 2026 Business Category Grant Schedule January Skilled Trades US$10,000 February Health Fitness US$10,000 March Food Beverage US$10,000 April Sustainability US$10,000 May Mental Emotional Support US$10,000 June Business Support Services US$10,000 July Animal Services US$10,000 August Hair Care Skincare US$10,000 September Education Child Care US$10,000 October Creative Arts US$10,000 November STEM US$10,000 December Fashion Interior Design US$10,000 Additional Benefit Each Business Category Grant winner automatically becomes eligible for the: US$50,000 Annual Business Category Grant 4. Annual Amber Grants At the end of each year, WomensNet awards three separate US$50,000 grants . These grants recognize the year s outstanding winners from: Monthly Amber Grants Monthly Startup Grants Business Category Grants This means that monthly winners have the opportunity to receive substantial additional funding without submitting another application. One Application for Multiple Grants One of the most valuable aspects of the WomensNet grant system is its streamlined application process. With a single application , applicants are automatically considered for: Monthly Amber Grant Monthly Startup Grant (if eligible) Monthly Business Category Grant (if eligible) Annual US$50,000 Grant opportunities This greatly increases the chances of receiving funding. Who Should Apply? The program is ideal for: Women launching new businesses. Entrepreneurs developing business ideas. Existing women-owned businesses seeking growth capital. Women-owned nonprofit organizations. Businesses operating in any industry. Entrepreneurs working within one of the featured annual business categories. Funding Summary Grant Funding Monthly Amber Grant US$10,000 Monthly Startup Grant US$10,000 Monthly Business Category Grant US$10,000 Annual Amber Grant US$50,000 (3 winners annually) Deadline There is no single annual deadline . Applications are accepted throughout the year, and applicants are considered according to the monthly grant cycle. Business Category Grant applications remain active for 12 months after submission. How to Apply Applicants only need to submit one application through the official WomensNet application portal to be considered for all eligible grants. Prospective applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible to maximize eligibility for monthly and annual funding opportunities. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Grants opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post WomensNet 2026 Grants: Apply for More Than $510,000 in Funding for Women-Owned Businesses Including $10,000 Monthly Grants and $50,000 Annual Grants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: OPCW Scholarships 2026 for the Asser–OPCW Training Programme on Disarmament and Non-Proliferation of Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD) in The Hague, Netherlands
summary: Professionals working in arms control, non-proliferation, international security, diplomacy, and chemical weapons policy now have an exceptional opportunity to strengthen their expertise through one of the world’s most prestigious disarmament training programmes. Applications are now open for the 2026 Asser–OPCW Training Programme on Disarmament and Non-Proliferation of Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD), jointly organized by the Asser Institute and the Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) in The Hague, Netherlands. As part of this initiative, the OPCW is offering tuition fee waiver scholarships funded by the European Union for selected participants working in civil society, NGOs, think tanks, and academia. The intensive five-day programme will take place from 26 October to 30 October 2026 and provides participants with advanced practical and policy-oriented training on the rapidly evolving global challenges surrounding weapons of mass destruction, arms control, export regulations, emerging technologies, and international law. Programme Overview The international arms control landscape is currently facing unprecedented pressure due to geopolitical fragmentation, technological disruption, and the weakening of global non-proliferation frameworks. Concerns over nuclear weapons have returned to global political discourse, chemical weapons continue to pose major threats in active conflicts, and emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and biotechnology are reshaping traditional security environments faster than existing legal systems can adapt. Against this backdrop, the Asser–OPCW Training Programme was designed to equip professionals with the legal, diplomatic, technical, and strategic knowledge required to navigate today’s complex disarmament and non-proliferation challenges. Participants will engage directly with leading international experts, practitioners, policymakers, and researchers through interactive lectures, discussions, case studies, and field visits. Scholarship Benefits Selected scholarship recipients will receive: Full tuition fee waiver worth €1,895 Access to expert-led sessions and workshops Networking opportunities with international security professionals Exposure to high-level practitioners in arms control and disarmament Participation in exclusive institutional field visits Advanced practical understanding of modern WMD challenges Professional development in international law, export controls, and security governance The scholarships are funded by the European Union and specifically support professionals from civil society organizations and academic sectors. Female applicants and candidates from lower-income countries are strongly encouraged to apply. Programme Dates and Location Dates: 26 October – 30 October 2026 Duration: Five days Venue: Asser Institute and OPCW Location: The Hague, Netherlands Address: R.J. Schimmelpennincklaan 20-22 2517 JN The Hague Netherlands Key Topics Covered During the Programme Participants will receive comprehensive training on some of the world’s most urgent disarmament and security challenges. 1. Nuclear Weapons and Strategic Stability Sessions will examine: Expanding nuclear stockpiles Declining arms control agreements Challenges facing the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) Deadlocked NPT Review Conferences Future pathways for nuclear disarmament 2. Chemical Weapons and Accountability Participants will explore: Use of chemical weapons in modern conflicts International accountability mechanisms Verification and compliance systems Challenges of misinformation and disinformation Enforcement of chemical weapons conventions 3. Biological Weapons and Synthetic Biology Key discussions include: Risks posed by synthetic biology Advances in pathogen engineering Weaknesses in the Biological Weapons Convention Biosecurity governance challenges Future biological weapons threats 4. Export Controls and Emerging Technologies The programme also addresses rapidly developing technologies, including: Artificial intelligence-enabled systems Autonomous weapons Hypersonic missile technology Export control regulations Legal and ethical gaps in emerging military technologies Exclusive Field Visits One of the most valuable components of the programme is direct institutional exposure through guided visits to internationally significant facilities. Participants will visit: OPCW Centre for Chemistry and Technology (ChemTech) This advanced facility supports scientific research, verification training, and international cooperation related to chemical weapons prohibition. OPCW Headquarters Participants will gain firsthand insight into the operations of one of the world’s leading international organizations dedicated to eliminating chemical weapons globally. Confirmed International Experts and Speakers The programme brings together highly respected scholars and practitioners from globally recognized institutions. Confirmed speakers include: Dr León Castellanos-Jankiewicz – Asser Institute Simon Cleobury – Geneva Centre for Security Policy Dr Sico van der Meer – Clingendael Institute Dr Oliver Meier – European Leadership Network Ionut Suseanu – International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Dr Ana Sánchez-Cobaleda – University of Barcelona Dr Sibylle Bauer – Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) Dr James Revill – United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) Dr Miranda Smith – SIPRI Dr Edward Howell – University of Oxford Dr Abdolrasool Farzam Divsallar – UNIDIR Who Should Apply? This programme is ideal for professionals and students seeking specialized expertise in international security and disarmament policy. Suitable applicants include: Government officials Diplomats NGO professionals Think tank researchers Academic scholars International organization staff Compliance officers Legal advisers Graduate students transitioning into the security field Applicants with backgrounds in international relations, law, diplomacy, political science, security studies, public policy, arms control, and strategic affairs are especially encouraged to apply. Why This Programme Matters The global security environment is evolving rapidly. Traditional arms control systems are increasingly challenged by geopolitical tensions, technological innovation, and the growing complexity of modern warfare. This programme provides participants with: Practical policy knowledge Exposure to real-world disarmament challenges International networking opportunities Institutional access to global security actors Specialized expertise highly valued in diplomacy and international organizations For professionals seeking careers in international security, arms control, diplomacy, compliance, or multilateral policy-making, this programme represents a significant career-development opportunity. Tuition Fee Standard Programme Fee: €1,895 Scholarship Support: Tuition fee waivers available through OPCW scholarships Application Deadline 1 August 2026 Learn More Apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the OPCW Scholarships 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . title: UNFPA Programme Specialist (P-3) Job in Ethiopia 2026: Apply for the Spotlight Initiative Africa Regional Programme 2.0 Before 14 July
summary: Professionals passionate about gender equality, women s rights, policy development, and international development now have an outstanding opportunity to contribute to one of Africa s most significant regional programmes addressing violence against women and girls. The United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) is inviting applications for the position of Programme Specialist (P-3) under the Spotlight Initiative Africa Regional Programme (SIARP) 2.0 , based in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia . This international professional position offers experienced development practitioners the opportunity to work closely with the African Union Commission (AUC) , United Nations agencies, Regional Economic Communities (RECs), civil society organizations, and development partners to strengthen regional efforts aimed at eliminating violence against women and girls, ending harmful practices, and advancing sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) across Africa. Application Deadline Deadline: 14 July 2026 Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the closing date. Job Overview Position: Programme Specialist Programme: Spotlight Initiative Africa Regional Programme (SIARP) 2.0 Organization: United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) Location: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia Grade: P-3 Job Type: Fixed-Term Contract Duration: One year, with the possibility of extension Work Schedule: Full-time Rotational Position: Yes About the Spotlight Initiative Africa Regional Programme 2.0 The Spotlight Initiative is one of the world s largest global partnerships dedicated to ending violence against women and girls. Funded by the European Union and implemented in partnership with United Nations agencies, the initiative works to eliminate gender-based violence while promoting gender equality and protecting the rights of women and girls across Africa. Building on the achievements of SIARP 1.0, the second phase focuses on strengthening regional collaboration through partnerships with: African Union Commission (AUC) United Nations agencies Regional Economic Communities (RECs) Civil Society Organizations Youth-led movements Women s rights organizations Development partners The programme adopts a comprehensive, rights-based, survivor-centred approach that addresses both the causes and consequences of violence against women and girls. About the Role The Programme Specialist will provide strategic leadership and technical support to the African Union Commission while ensuring regional programmes align with international standards and continental priorities. The successful candidate will play a central role in coordinating stakeholders, strengthening partnerships, promoting policy reforms, supporting advocacy initiatives, and ensuring effective implementation of SIARP 2.0. This position is designed for professionals capable of managing large-scale regional programmes involving multiple partners across Africa. Key Responsibilities The selected Programme Specialist will contribute to several strategic areas of the programme. 1. Programme Coordination Responsibilities include: Coordinating implementation across partner organizations. Supporting strategic planning. Monitoring programme performance. Providing technical guidance to implementing partners. Identifying programme risks and recommending solutions. 2. Strategic Partnerships The successful candidate will: Collaborate with the African Union Commission. Work closely with UNDP, UNICEF, UN Women, and UNFPA. Strengthen partnerships with Regional Economic Communities. Engage civil society organizations. Collaborate with youth groups and women s rights organizations. Foster relationships with traditional and religious leaders. 3. Policy Development The Programme Specialist will support: Alignment of African regional policies with international standards. Strengthening legal and institutional frameworks. Promotion of gender equality policies. Elimination of harmful practices. Advancement of sexual and reproductive health and rights. 4. Capacity Building Responsibilities include: Identifying institutional capacity gaps. Designing and facilitating training programmes. Mentoring programme partners. Supporting cross-learning initiatives. Developing technical guidance materials. 5. Advocacy and Communications The role includes: Developing communication strategies. Preparing advocacy materials. Supporting regional awareness campaigns. Coordinating programme visibility. Producing knowledge products. Promoting digital innovation in advocacy. 6. Knowledge Management The Programme Specialist will: Document programme lessons. Promote evidence-based policymaking. Coordinate knowledge sharing among partners. Disseminate regional best practices. Support monitoring and evaluation systems. Programme Objectives SIARP 2.0 focuses on four major outcomes: Strengthening legal, policy, institutional, and data systems addressing violence against women and girls. Promoting prevention strategies that challenge discriminatory gender norms. Improving access to survivor-centred services for victims of gender-based violence. Strengthening women s rights organizations and youth-led movements to advocate for accountability and lasting change. Required Qualifications Applicants must possess: A Master s degree in a relevant discipline. At least five years of progressively responsible professional experience. Demonstrated experience managing complex international programmes. Experience working with governments, donors, and civil society. Strong analytical and policy development skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Strong leadership and stakeholder engagement abilities. Excellent organizational and project management skills. Preferred Experience Candidates with the following experience will have an advantage: Previous work with the United Nations. Experience with the African Union Commission. Gender equality programming. Violence against women and girls (VAWG) initiatives. Sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) programming. Regional policy development. Results-based programme management. Required Competencies Successful applicants should demonstrate: Strategic thinking Accountability Integrity Professionalism Teamwork Advocacy skills Partnership building Policy analysis Communication excellence Programme leadership Results-based management Creative problem-solving Commitment to diversity and inclusion Language Requirements Applicants must have: Fluency in English. Working knowledge of French is considered an advantage. Benefits of Working with UNFPA Working with UNFPA provides an opportunity to contribute directly to sustainable development and gender equality while enjoying a competitive international employment package. Benefits include: Competitive international salary. Health insurance. Additional UN staff benefits where applicable. Professional development opportunities. International networking. Meaningful work supporting gender equality and human rights. Collaborative multicultural work environment. Opportunity to influence continental policy and regional development. Why You Should Apply This position offers a rare opportunity to work at the intersection of international development, gender equality, public policy, and regional cooperation. The successful candidate will help shape continental strategies, strengthen partnerships across Africa, and contribute to eliminating violence against women and girls while advancing sustainable development goals. Professionals seeking to make a lasting impact in international development and human rights should strongly consider this opportunity. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their application through the official UNFPA application portal before the deadline. title: Explore 12 Free FAO eLearning Academy Courses on Sustainability, Food Security, Nutrition, Biodiversity, Forestry, and Agriculture in 2026
summary: The global transition toward more sustainable food systems requires skilled professionals, informed policymakers, empowered communities, and knowledgeable young leaders. To support this transformation, the FAO eLearning Academy has launched an impressive collection of free online sustainability courses covering critical topics ranging from biodiversity and climate-smart agriculture to food loss reduction, nutrition, gender equality, agroforestry, and responsible investment. These self-paced online courses have been designed by experts from the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) of the United Nations to strengthen knowledge, develop practical skills, and support sustainable development worldwide. Whether you are a student, researcher, government official, development practitioner, nutritionist, farmer, environmental expert, or simply passionate about sustainable food systems, these learning opportunities provide valuable knowledge completely free of charge. In addition to the online courses, FAO is also inviting young people to participate in its Global Youth Contest: From Learning to Impact , providing an exciting opportunity to showcase innovative ideas and contribute to sustainable development. Why Enroll in FAO eLearning Academy Courses? The FAO eLearning Academy has become one of the world s leading platforms for capacity building in agriculture, food security, nutrition, climate resilience, and rural development. Participants benefit from: Free access to world-class learning resources. Self-paced online study. Courses developed by international experts. Practical knowledge applicable to real-world challenges. Global perspectives on sustainable development. Learning materials aligned with international standards. Opportunities to strengthen professional skills. Enhanced career prospects in agriculture, nutrition, environmental management, and international development. Complete List of Available FAO Sustainability Courses 1. Global Youth Contest: From Learning to Impact The Global Youth Contest encourages young people to transform the knowledge gained through FAO learning into practical solutions that address global food security, sustainability, and environmental challenges. Participants have the opportunity to showcase innovative ideas that can create positive impact in their communities and beyond. Deadline: 31 July 2026 2. Multidimensional Solutions to Reduce Food Loss and Waste in Fish Value Chains Food loss and waste remain major global challenges affecting food security and economic development. This course explores: Causes of food loss in fisheries Sustainable fish value chains Waste reduction strategies Policy interventions Best practices across the fisheries sector 3. Costing and Monetizing Benefits for Nutrition This course introduces participants to economic approaches used to evaluate nutrition investments. Topics include: Cost-benefit analysis Economic valuation Nutrition investment planning Measuring returns on nutrition interventions Evidence-based policy development 4. Biodiversity and Agrifood Systems – Introduction Biodiversity is essential for resilient food systems. Participants learn about: Biodiversity conservation Ecosystem services Sustainable agriculture Food system resilience Environmental sustainability 5. Introduction to Documenting Plant Genetic Resources for Food and Agriculture Plant genetic resources are fundamental to global food security. This course explains: Plant genetic diversity Documentation methods Conservation systems Germplasm management Agricultural biodiversity 6. Voluntary Guidelines on Gender Equality and Women s and Girls Empowerment in the Context of Food Security and Nutrition Gender equality plays a central role in achieving sustainable development. The course covers: Women s empowerment Food security Gender-responsive policies Inclusive agriculture Nutrition governance International voluntary guidelines 7. Agrifood Systems Solutions for Halting Deforestation Agricultural expansion remains one of the leading causes of deforestation. Participants explore: Sustainable land management Deforestation prevention Responsible supply chains Environmental governance Climate change mitigation 8. Trade and Nutrition: Policy Coherence for Healthy Diets Trade policies directly influence food availability and nutrition outcomes. Topics include: Nutrition-sensitive trade policies Healthy diets Policy coherence Food systems governance International trade frameworks 9. Farmers Markets: An Introduction Farmers markets strengthen local food systems and rural economies. This introductory course discusses: Local food markets Market organization Value chain development Farmer participation Consumer engagement Sustainable local economies 10. Empower Women to Engage in Responsible Investment in Agriculture and Food Systems Women s participation in agricultural investment is essential for inclusive growth. Participants will learn about: Responsible agricultural investment Gender-inclusive finance Women s economic empowerment Sustainable agribusiness Food systems development 11. Agroforestry for Sustainable Agrifood Transformation – Introduction Agroforestry integrates trees into agricultural landscapes to improve productivity and sustainability. The course examines: Agroforestry systems Climate resilience Soil conservation Biodiversity Sustainable farming Carbon sequestration 12. Enhancing Policy Coherence Through Public Expenditure Analysis for Forests Effective forest governance depends on sound public investment. Topics include: Forest financing Public expenditure analysis Policy coherence Sustainable forest management Governance tools Budget planning Who Should Take These Courses? These learning opportunities are highly relevant for: University students Graduate students Researchers Nutritionists Agricultural extension workers Forestry professionals Environmental scientists Government officials Policymakers NGO professionals International development practitioners Food systems experts Climate professionals Sustainable development advocates Skills You Can Develop Completing these courses can help participants strengthen knowledge in: Sustainable agriculture Food security Nutrition economics Biodiversity conservation Agroforestry Forestry governance Responsible investment Gender equality Climate resilience Food systems transformation Agricultural policy Public expenditure analysis Trade policy Fisheries management Why These Courses Matter The world is facing interconnected challenges including climate change, biodiversity loss, food insecurity, malnutrition, deforestation, and unsustainable agricultural practices. Building knowledge and professional capacity is essential for addressing these issues. The FAO eLearning Academy equips learners with internationally recognized knowledge that supports the achievement of multiple Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), including Zero Hunger, Climate Action, Life on Land, Gender Equality, and Responsible Consumption and Production. Whether your goal is career advancement, academic development, policy implementation, or community impact, these free courses offer valuable learning opportunities from one of the world s leading organizations in agriculture and food security. Deadline Most of the online courses are available on a rolling basis. However, the Global Youth Contest: From Learning to Impact has an application deadline of 31 July 2026 .
